title: lupinus bartlettianus
intro: Lupinus bartlettianus, also known as Bartlett's lupine, is a perennial plant species in the genus Lupinus, a group of flowering plants commonly known as lupines. It is native to the southwestern United States and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Lupinus bartlettianus include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Lupinus bartlettianus can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and crushing the plant material to make capsules or tinctures.
